category_title: Struggling readers who view reading as a process of remembering and repeating
scenario_title: Scripted Phonics Intervention and Nonsense Words
minimal_context: Alyssa is participating in a scripted phonics drill and reading nonsense syllables for a weekly assessment in a small Title I group.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: [points to o] Here is a new sound. My turn: /o/. Your turn. Get ready.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: /o/ [holding sound until the tutor lifts their finger] Last night, I found a—

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Let's look at the assessment now. Please read this syllable: lom.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Let's try the next one on the list: af.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: [points to o] Here is a new sound. My turn: /o/. Your turn. Get ready.

STUDENT: /o/ [holding sound until the tutor lifts their finger] Last night, I found a—

TUTOR: Let's look at the assessment now. Please read this syllable: lom.

STUDENT: lock... lock... lock-m.

TUTOR: Let's try the next one on the list: af.

STUDENT: Is af an animal?

task.content: Participating in a scripted intensive intervention program involving step-by-step instruction and repetition of letter sounds, and attempting to read a nonsense syllable ('lom') during a weekly literacy assessment
task.learning_goal: repetition of letter sounds (specifically the sound /o/)

context.social_setting: small group intensive intervention with a classmate
context.time_context: weekly assessment and scripted phonics drill

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: When presented with nonsense syllables during scripted Title I instruction or weekly assessment
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: persistent in her attempts to impose meaning on some of the texts... read 'lock,' 'lock,' 'lock-m.' and questioning the meaning of a nonsense syllable
challenging_behavior_trigger.internal_state: persistent in her attempts to impose meaning on some of the texts

behavior_patterns.condition: During a scripted phonics drill in Title I
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: attempting to share a personal narrative
